item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It said case 2 was ones that died in the hospital but it also said development stopped at three months of age AND the same as the baby reached last time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like, it could do nothing but….what if one or more spirits of the baby's decide to feed on it like a parasite before replacing it. I just gave shivers down my own spine

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just thought of a scary thought but

What if an already pregnant female entered the area?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came across this one a few weeks ago, and I'll admit, there's still something about it that hasn't been completely "unlocked" yet. I pondered on this for a while, until a couple of questions hit me which the answers might turn up the creepy level on this one.

1. How did this place come to the attention of the Foundation? Who were the first victims? In other words, what poor girl went into that hospital on a silly dare, and had to suffer the effects of the anomaly? Did she survive? If so, was she ever the same afterwards?

2. I also wondered, these case threes…do they have any emotional attachment to anyone? Do they have any emotional attachment to their (most recent) mother? What if they didn't?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an ok scip, just a little sterile (pardon the expression). I don't really feel the anima and I'm not connected to the story (the hook is a little loose). The horror is what happens in the hospital and that's completely skipped over (whatever it is it's a little fucked-up). I don't think you'd want to go full Montauk on this, but I would be interested in a little more 'shock'.

Or maybe the human perspective. Like the mothers become obsessed with their 'sweet little baby boy, their precious'. Perhaps an interview with one of these obsessed mothers (although, this seems a little obvious to me).

It's a descent scip, which is well written. There's not a good twist to land me on my head, and it's not twisted enough to keep me up at night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the only [DATA EXPUNGED] I use in any of my articles, actually. All other redaction is just names, dates, and places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I, for one, like this…In particular, this article provides a perfect example of the proper use of "[DATA EXPUNGED]" - the expunged bit is the one thing in the article that's really a lot worse left to the imagination than told, no matter how imaginative the reader might be. I'd upvote for that alone, but this is a good, anomalous scip overall as well. Kudos. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Elaborate. Too weak of a hook? What do you feel might improve the article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could have gone far with this one, but it just doesn't grab me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just fails to do anything for me. The slacking of technical language, the somewhat uninteresting effects…it just feels like a "sure it's an anomaly, but so what?" The "hook" of the Class Threes also seems off - there seems to be no real reason to only allow certain people access, and it isn't made to seem "secret" in any other way, not even a bit of JavaScript to hide the section in a link. It's just…milquetoast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh.

I finally understand why the Class 3 babies are there.

They've been robbed of a life, just not physically. They haven't died, but they still aren't living a life.

…I think I may have to sob in the corner now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: perhaps add a bit to the psychological disorders: "Antisocial Personality Disorder, sociopathy and/or paranoid/persecutory delusions"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took a bit of advice from some people on this thread. Updated it with Case 3, which is somewhat different than what was suggested.
Hopefully, this counts as a hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just a nitpick, but maternity wards in the first world don't look like that. That's likely either an ER, a post-op care ward, or possibly a cardiac monitoring ward. As a hospital employee, that inconsistency really jarred my suspension of disbelief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if whatever… thing… made this made a mistake. And trapped the ghost of a baby that didn't actually die. Suddenly, you have a baby that wasn't robbed of life at a young age, but a baby who wasn't but is still grouped with all these others…

Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except that that doesn't make sense in the context of the premise. It's the ghosts of the babies that never got a chance at life, not simply ones that the hospital didn't list as alive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the baby LIVES. But it's not in any hospital records. It could do a lot of things, like being a serial killer, an actual person who is still alive, or could simply kill the mother in childbirth… hm…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that this is missing something. I have no idea what a Class Three could be, though. I can see that this is close to going over the edge into really good, but unfortunately I can't quite see how.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With an extra paragraph, this could be creepy as fuck. Adding something about Class Threes will probably do the trick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm on the fence about it.
On one hand, there's some real potential here in my opinion, especially with the whole "eternally pregnant" bit.
On the other hand, while it's definitely strange, there's very little creepy\harmful\squicky here. I suggest thinking about several shudder-worthy answers to questions already brought up here - what happens if the woman is already pregnant? What happens after reallyl ong drawn-out pregnancies? How exactly do subjects react to suggestions\attempts at abortion? What happens when a fmealte-too-male transgendered subject is introduced into the SCP?

There's a lot of untapped bodyhorror here. Downvoting for now, will upvoted if the article is improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about this one. It's not creppy, but there's something just offputting about it… Of course, there's a simple way to deal with the babies that are Case 2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The spectrail wailing-and-crying is heard even by clinically deaf women?

Are congenitally-deaf women immune to this because, having been deaf from birth, they're unable to recognize the weird stimulus appearing in their brains?

And what exactly counts as "fertile"? Is it safe for a woman to enter if she's already pregnant? What if she's had a tubal ligation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the issue is there's a feeling of "and then what happens" when you get to the end of it. Like there's supposed to be just a bit more weirdness, something to help explain how it started, or to show what happens next.

Also, in my opinion "…and a fence erected around the building." should probably be "…and a fence maintained around the building."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is currently my worst ranked article. And I can see why. It's certainly missing something, but for the life of me, I can't see what that is or how to improve this. Any thoughts on how I can make this better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. I second the not scary, not creepy, but I'm not finding anything appealing about it. They're just normal fetuses/babies, aside from not aging, and since that doesn't bother the parents… I just am not finding the horror in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It's not scary, it's not terribly creepy, but I feel horrible for anyone who interacts with this SCP. This is one of the better gut-punches on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They just want another chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The health risks are no more or less than any comparable pregnancy, just perhaps intensified by being drawn out longer. How they react probably varies a fair bit from woman to woman, as it's not like it's mind control or anything.

DrSevere: Did you note how it's actually two puns? "Eternity Ward" sounds like "Maternity Ward", and if you get affected by it, you have to take care of something entirely dependent on you forever: a ward for eternity if you will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Eternity Ward"?

/me calls the pun police

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*inexperienced poster alert*

On the whole, I'd say this is an interesting article, but it doesn't really grab me.

Ugh, I'm not good at this whole "suggestions" thing, but I'll try and be a bit more specific here. Isn't that nice of me?

Erhm, right. Moving on.

I guess what I think this needs really is development. As a young woman, I'm not thrilled by the idea of heading into a hospital and getting impregnated with multiple fetuses that may never be born or never progress beyond a certain stage, but what you have here doesn't exactly fill me with dread, either.

I'd suggest developing both the two types of pregnancies - What exactly happens when the children stop development in utero? What are the health risks to the women carrying them? - and the bit you mentioned about the women being happy with this state of affairs. Do they react violently to attempts to terminate the pregnancies, or separate them from the baby? Do they respond to the death/removal of the child(ren) as if it had been a normal pregnancy? What about the emotional toll of bonding to a child that will never walk, talk, or call them "Mommy"? Do they attempt to repeat the experience, even if they had a strongly negative one?

Yeah, there's a reason I don't write my own SCPs. I think this one has potential, even if I'm not sure how to bring it out.

*Return to your normally scheduled comments*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this is my entry for the contest. Basically, I got the inspiration from the alleged existence of an urban legend about a hospital haunted by babies. But how would babies haunt? I figured that they'd want what was denied to them: a life. I basically spun the idea out from there. I'm reasonably happy with what I've turned out with, though if you have any suggestions, please make your voice heard.
